summary: Reintroduced lbArchive_LoadSections in src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c. The restored helper takes an HSD_Archive plus varargs pairs of destination symbol pointers and public symbol names, looks each name up with HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, stores the result through the caller-provided void**, and reports missing symbols with OSReport. The PR had no body or review discussion, so the exact regression or caller need is not documented beyond the title.
what_changed: Added lbArchive_LoadSections(HSD_Archive* archive, void** symbol, ...) to lbarchive.c.
The function initializes a va_list with va_start(symbols, symbol).
It iterates until the destination pointer argument is NULL.

For each entry, it reads a const char* symbol_name from the varargs, clears *symbol to NULL, assigns *symbol = HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(archive, symbol_name), and reports failure with OSReport("Cannot find symbol %s.\n", symbol_name).
The loop advances to the next destination pointer with va_arg(symbols, void**), then calls va_end(symbols) after the loop.
No files besides src/melee/lb/lbarchive.c were changed, and there were no deletions.
smart_moves: Restored the helper as a compact varargs API rather than duplicating repeated public-symbol lookup code at each archive load site.
Preserved the destination-pointer/symbol-name pairing pattern: each void** output is paired with a const char* public symbol name.
Explicitly clears the output pointer to NULL before attempting the lookup, leaving a safe failure state if the symbol cannot be found.
Uses OSReport for missing public symbols, which makes archive symbol failures visible without changing the function signature.
decomp_lessons: For lbarchive helpers, the varargs order matters: initial argument is a void** destination, followed by repeated const char* symbol names and next void** destinations, ending when the destination pointer is NULL.

When reintroducing previously removed helper functions, a small source-level restoration can matter for compatibility with callers or expected project APIs even if no matching assembly changes are shown in the slice.
Archive public-symbol loaders should preserve the NULL-on-failure behavior and the diagnostic OSReport text if future matching work depends on emitted strings or call structure.
This PR provides limited rationale because there was no PR body, issue discussion, review, or comments in the slice; future researchers should verify callers and declarations separately.
naming_conventions: Function follows the existing library prefix and CamelCase style: lbArchive_LoadSections.
Local names are descriptive and type-oriented: symbol, symbols, symbol_name.
Uses HSD_Archive and HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress naming for archive-level public symbol access.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Varargs helpers require matching the exact va_arg sequence and sentinel loop shape; here the loop checks the current void** symbol for NULL, reads a const char* name, performs the lookup, then reads the next void**.
The duplicated assignment pattern `*symbol = NULL; *symbol = HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress(...)` may be intentional for matching or behavior and should not be simplified without checking output.
The exact diagnostic string `Cannot find symbol %s.\n` is part of the restored function and can be a useful anchor when comparing against binaries or prior source.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body text were present in the provided slice.
